questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Massachusetts licensee complete before their first license renewal?
distractors: 8 hours
16 hours
24 hours
questions.explanation: Massachusetts requires 12 hours of CE before the first license renewal. CE must be completed BEFORE renewing to maintain active status.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to renew as active but has only completed 8 of the required 12 CE hours. What happens?
questions.correct_answer: The license is renewed as inactive
distractors: The renewal is denied entirely
A 90-day grace period is granted
The license is suspended for 6 months
questions.explanation: If CE is not fully completed before renewal, the license is renewed as inactive. The licensee cannot practice until completing the remaining CE hours and reactivating.

questions.question_text: Continuing education courses for Massachusetts licensees must be approved by which entity?
distractors: The National Association of REALTORS
The Massachusetts Bar Association
Any accredited university
questions.explanation: CE courses must be approved by the Board of Registration of Real Estate Brokers and Salespersons. Only Board-approved courses count toward the renewal requirement.

questions.question_text: A licensee completed CE after their renewal date but before practicing again. Can they reactivate their license?
distractors: No, the license is permanently expired
Only by retaking the licensing exam
Only within 30 days of the renewal date
questions.explanation: An inactive license can be reactivated once the licensee completes the required CE hours. The license is not permanently lost, but no practice is permitted while inactive.

questions.question_text: When must a Massachusetts licensee complete their CE hours relative to their renewal date?
distractors: Within 60 days after renewal
Within 90 days after renewal
Within the calendar year of renewal
questions.explanation: CE must be completed BEFORE the renewal date to renew as active. There is no grace period — failure to complete CE before renewal results in inactive status.

questions.question_text: A Massachusetts salesperson changes their home address. They must notify which entity?
distractors: Their affiliated broker only
The local Registry of Deeds
The Division of Banks
questions.explanation: Licensees must notify the Board of Registration of any change of address. This is one of several mandatory Board notification requirements.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A to affiliate with Broker B. Who must notify the Board of this change?
questions.correct_answer: Both the salesperson and the brokers
distractors: Only the salesperson leaving the firm
Only the new Broker B receiving them
Only the former Broker A releasing them
questions.explanation: Salespersons must notify the Board of any change in broker affiliation. Brokers must also notify the Board of new affiliations and terminations of salespersons.

questions.question_text: A Massachusetts licensee is convicted of fraud in another state. Must they report this to the Board?
distractors: No, only MA convictions matter
Only if it involves real estate
Only if the other state revokes their license
questions.explanation: Licensees must notify the Board of any criminal conviction or disciplinary action in another jurisdiction. The Board considers out-of-state conduct relevant to fitness to practice.

questions.question_text: A Massachusetts license expires on the licensee's date of birth. The license term ranges from:
distractors: 1 to 2 years
Exactly 2 years
3 to 4 years
questions.explanation: The license issue date is when the candidate passed the exam, and expiration is their birthday. This creates a term of no less than 2 years and no more than 3 years.

questions.question_text: A broker terminates a salesperson for trust account violations. The broker must notify:
distractors: The local police department
The salesperson's next broker
No one, it is a private matter
questions.explanation: Brokers must notify the Board of terminations of affiliated salespersons. This is especially important when the termination involves potential violations of law or regulation.

questions.question_text: A real estate agent licensed in Connecticut wants to practice in Massachusetts. What is the MOST accurate statement?
questions.correct_answer: They may apply for a waiver of education requirements
distractors: They can immediately practice in MA
They must complete 40 hours of MA education
MA does not allow out-of-state licensees
questions.explanation: Out-of-state licensees may apply with a letter of good standing for a waiver of the 40-hour education requirement. However, they must still pass both portions of the MA exam.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state licensee obtains a waiver of the education requirement. What must they still do before practicing in Massachusetts?
questions.correct_answer: Pass both portions of the MA exam
distractors: Pass only the state portion of the exam
Complete 20 hours of MA-specific education
Submit a surety bond to the Board
questions.explanation: Even with an education waiver, out-of-state applicants must pass both the national and state portions of the Massachusetts licensing exam. The waiver only covers the 40-hour education requirement.

questions.question_text: To qualify for a Massachusetts education waiver, an out-of-state licensee must provide:
questions.correct_answer: A letter of good standing from their state
distractors: Proof of 5 years of active experience
A surety bond filed with the Board
An endorsement from a licensed MA broker
questions.explanation: Out-of-state applicants seeking an education waiver must provide a letter of good standing, and their license in the other state must be current at the time of application.

questions.question_text: Massachusetts evaluates out-of-state license applications on what basis?
distractors: Automatic reciprocity with all states
Reciprocity only with New England states
Blanket denial of all out-of-state licenses
questions.explanation: The Board evaluates out-of-state licenses on a case-by-case basis. Massachusetts does not have formal reciprocity agreements with specific states.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state licensee's home state license has expired. Can they receive a Massachusetts education waiver?
questions.correct_answer: No, the out-of-state license must be current
distractors: Yes, if expired within 2 years
Yes, with proof of prior licensure
Yes, if they have 5+ years experience
questions.explanation: The license in the other state must be current for a waiver to be issued. An expired license does not qualify for the education waiver; the applicant would need to complete the full 40 hours.
